title: Multiscale & Multiphysics Modeling
description: We develop multiscale workflows that connect simulation and experiments for process design and device-level performance prediction across energy and functional materials systems.
focus: This topic focuses on three connected directions: simulation-guided dry-electrode process design, hybridization of sparse experiments with large simulation data for manufacturing optimization, and cross-scale electromechanical modeling for flexible energy-harvesting structures.
methods: Hybrid Data Integration: Combine limited experimental data with large simulation datasets to improve model robustness and reduce trial-and-error in process design.
Process-Scale Modeling: Build multiscale representations of solvent-free dry electrode fabrication to link processing parameters with resulting electrode microstructures and performance.
Device-Scale Multiphysics: Use multiscale electromechanical simulations to quantify flexoelectric and piezoelectric contributions in core-shell nanostructures for energy harvesters.
deliverables: Process Design Rules: Actionable guidelines for selecting dry-electrode processing conditions based on predicted structure-performance relationships.
Data-Efficient Optimization: Practical workflows for combining experiments and simulations in ceramic/process development with better data efficiency.
Performance Insights: Quantitative design insights for enhancing output in flexible piezoelectric energy-harvesting architectures.

relatedPapers.title: Multiscale insights enable rational design of solvent-free dry electrode processing for advanced battery electrode fabrication
A versatile strategy for hybridizing small experimental and large simulation data: A case for ceramic tape-casting process

title: Nucleation Theory
description: We use nucleation-centered frameworks to predict phase formation pathways, impurity evolution, and growth behavior under realistic synthesis conditions.
focus: This topic targets phase-selection control by combining thermodynamic driving-force analysis, reaction-network interpretation, and growth-condition optimization for oxide and solid-electrolyte systems.
methods: Reaction Path Analysis: Track feasible intermediate pathways to identify synthesis routes with low impurity risk and high phase selectivity.
Thermodynamic Screening: Evaluate stability windows and competing phase formation across composition and processing conditions.
Growth-Condition Optimization: Link transport and reaction conditions to phase evolution behavior for practical process design.
deliverables: Pathway Guidance: Ranked synthesis pathways for impurity-minimized target-phase formation.
Phase-Control Maps: Condition-dependent maps for stable/metastable regime selection and process tuning.
Scale-Up Rules: Actionable condition-selection guidelines for reproducible growth and synthesis optimization.

title: AI-assisted Synthesis Recipes
description: We build AI-assisted synthesis-recipe workflows by combining curated literature, simulation-derived descriptors, and data-driven models for practical materials discovery.
focus: This topic centers on three tasks: extracting actionable synthesis knowledge from prior studies, building predictive models for synthesizability and property trends, and converting model outputs into practical recipe recommendations.
methods: Knowledge Structuring: Organize literature and project data into machine-readable synthesis variables and outcome labels.
Modeling for Synthesizability: Train and validate data-driven models to predict feasible composition/process windows and likely synthesis success.
Design-Space Prioritization: Use AI predictions to rank candidate recipes and reduce experimental search cost.
deliverables: Recipe Candidates: Ranked synthesis conditions and composition candidates for targeted materials systems.
Decision Support: Interpretable indicators linking input variables to expected synthesis outcomes and performance trends.
Faster Iteration: Closed-loop workflow templates that shorten the cycle between prediction and experimental validation.

title: Semiconductor Materials and Processing
description: We model and optimize semiconductor materials and fabrication conditions to improve mobility, stability, and process reproducibility in oxide and wide-bandgap systems.
focus: This topic integrates data-driven analysis, thin-film process engineering, and epitaxial growth optimization to connect process conditions with device-relevant electronic performance.
methods: Data-Driven Assessment: Quantitative analysis of mobility-stability trade-offs in oxide semiconductors using curated publication and project data.
Thin-Film Process Control: Process-property modeling for atomically ordered oxide transistor channels, including deposition-window optimization.
Growth Simulation: CFD-supported optimization of HVPE growth conditions for uniform Ga2O3 film formation.
deliverables: Performance Trade-off Maps: Interpretable mobility-stability trend maps for oxide semiconductor design decisions.
Process Windows: Practical deposition and growth condition guidelines linked to target electronic properties.
Reproducibility Support: Condition-selection rules for uniform film quality and robust device-level performance.

title: Battery Materials and Processing
description: We combine electrochemical, mechanical, and process modeling to design safer, higher-performance battery materials and manufacturing routes.
focus: This topic connects electrode/electrolyte material behavior with process-level control, with emphasis on dry electrode fabrication, interfacial stabilization for lithium metal systems, and structural design for improved reversibility.
methods: Process-Aware Modeling: Analyze how fabrication conditions shape electrode microstructure and downstream electrochemical performance.
Interface Engineering Simulation: Evaluate coating and interfacial design strategies for lithium metal and all-solid-state battery stability.
Coupled Performance Analysis: Quantify electrochemical reversibility and degradation trends using structure-informed multiscale models.
deliverables: Manufacturing Windows: Actionable dry-process condition maps linked to target electrode performance.
Stability Guidelines: Design principles for chemically inert interfacial layers and reduced side-reaction risk.
Reversibility Improvement Paths: Practical structural optimization directions for longer cycle life and more stable lithium behavior.
